properties.remark: MESONET STATION CRII1 COTTON RIDGE.
MESONET STATION RFTI1 RAFT RIVER RAWS.
68 MPH WIND GUST MEASURED AT THE POCATELLO AIRPORT ASOS FROM A CONVECTIVE SQUALL LINE.
I-15 CLOSED FROM IDAHO FALLS/US-20 NORTH TO ID-33 DUE TO SEVERE BLOWING DUST WITH ZERO VISIBILITY. CLOSURE BEGAN JUST BEFORE 1 PM AND CONTINUED UNTIL JUST AFTER 11 PM.
SIGNIFICANTLY REDUCED VISIBILITY ALONG I-15 NEAR FORT HALL DUE TO BLOWING DUST. STRONG WINDS ALSO BLEW OVER A SEMI TRACTOR TRAILER AROUND 5:45 PM IN THIS AREA.

MOTORISTS REPORTED TO THE IDAHO STATE JOURNAL ZERO VISIBILITY CONDITIONS ON ID-39 BETWEEN ABERDEEN AND AMERICAN FALLS BECAUSE OF BLOWING DUST THAT CAUSED NUMEROUS WRECKS ON THE ROAD STARTING AROUND 530 PM. NO SERIOUS INJURIES REPORTED.

INTERSTATE 15 CLOSED BETWEEN HIGHWAY 20 IN IDAHO FALLS AND HIGHWAY 33 AT SAGE JUNCTION DUE TO A DUST STORM. AN ITD RWIS SENSOR NEAR OSGOOD RECORDED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 67 MPH AND VISIBILITY AS LOW AS 30 FEET DURING THE HEIGHT OF THE STORM. AS OF 1145PM MDT, THE INTERSTATE REMAINED CLOSED.
KPIH POCATELLO AIRPORT.
AROUND 5 PM, ROOF AND TREE DAMAGE.
OUTFLOW WINDS WITH BLOWING DUST REDUCING VISIBILITY TO AROUND 1/4 MILE OR LESS.
DIME-SIZED HAIL. 45-50 MPH WIND GUSTS. VERY HEAVY RAIN FOR 5 MINUTES.
SKYWARN SPOTTER REPORTS MOSTLY DIME SIZE HAIL AT RESIDENCE JUST NNW OF MOORE, WITH SEVERAL STONES ESTIMATED AT QUARTER SIZE. ALSO GUSTY WINDS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

PUBLIC PROVIDED VIDEO OF A DEBRIS FLOW ACROSS BOTH NORTHBOUND LANES OF INTERSTATE 15 JUST NORTH OF THE UTAH BORDER WITH WATER, MUD, AND LOGS ON THE ROADWAY FROM THUNDERSTORM RUNOFF. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR AND TIME OF VIDEO SUBMISSION.
MESONET STATION TAB.
NORTHWEST EDGE OF ASHTON. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF 10TH ST AND ID-47. PHOTOS AND VIDEOS SUBMITTED VIA SOCIAL MEDIA WITH LARGEST STONES ESTIMATED AT PING PONG BALL SIZE. ADDITIONAL PUBLIC REPORT/PHOTO OF QUARTER TO HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL SEVERAL BLOCKS AWAY. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
DIME TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL REPORTED.
DUBOIS ARL SITE.
CHINA POINT ITD SITE.

FALL RIVER RURAL ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE REPORTS THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW DOWN A TREE ON A MAIN TRANSMISSION LINE JUST SOUTH OF MACKS INN, KNOCKING OUT POWER TO THE ISLAND PARK AREA. SOME OUTAGES LASTED UNTIL AFTER 11 PM.

SEVERAL INCHES OF MUD, ROCKS AND DEBRIS OVER BAKER CREEK ROAD THAT CLOSED DOWN THE ROAD. THIS WAS IN AN AREA OF PREVIOUS FIRE BURN AND THE FIRST OF TWO SLIDE EVENTS THAT CLOSED DOWN THE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

SIGNIFICANT FLASH FLOODING ACROSS A GRAVEL ROAD, AND WATER OVERTOPPING A SMALL BRIDGE ALONG A PAVED ROAD, IN THE AREA OF S 2950 E AND HEGLAR CANYON RD. PHOTOS AND VIDEOS SUBMITTED TO NWS POCATELLO VIA TWITTER STARTING AROUND 320 PM AND CONTINUING AT 451 PM.

FLOODING OBSERVED ALONG TRAPPER CREEK ROAD WITH EXCESSIVE RUNOFF FROM THE SQUAW CREEK AND LITTLE SQUAW CREEK DRAINAGES. THIS LED TO SEVERAL INCHES OF ROCKS, MUD AND DEBRIS OVER THE ROADWAY. INFORMATION GAINED BY NWS SURVEY DISCUSSING WITH LOCAL LAND OWNER.
AN NWS SURVEY FOUND SEVERAL WASHOUTS ALONG HIGHWAY 37 SOUTH OF ROCKLAND INCLUDING ONE ALONG KING LANE. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
AN NWS SURVEY FOUND EVIDENCE OF HIGH WATER ALONG SEVERAL STRETCHES OF ARBON VALLEY ROAD. ROAD AND BRIDGE CREWS WERE ACTIVELY WORKING ON CLEANING UP DEBRIS. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

NWS SURVEY FOUND SEVERAL AREAS WHERE WATER HAD BEEN OVER HIGHWAY 36 AND DAMAGE TO CULVERTS WAS ALSO EVIDENT. IDAHO TRANSPORTATION DEPARTMENT WERE ACTIVELY CLEANING UP THE REMNANTS. TIME ESTIMATE VIA RADAR.

USFS REPORTED TRAPPER CREEK ROAD (533) WASHED OUT IN THE AREA OF FS ROAD 538 TO TROUT CREEK PASS. USFS OFFICIALS REPORTED A 12 FOOT DEEP GULLY RUNNING DOWN THE CENTER OF THE ROAD. THIS IS DUE TO EXCESSIVE RUNOFF FROM THE BURNED CANYON WALLS FROM THE BADGER BURN SCAR. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

SEVERAL INCHES OF MUD, ROCKS AND DEBRIS OVER BAKER CREEK ROAD THAT CLOSED DOWN THE ROAD. A SURVEY TEAM FOUND THIS WAS IN AN AREA OF PREVIOUS FIRE BURN AND IN DISCUSSIONS WITH LOCAL OFFICIALS, IT WAS DETERMINED THIS WAS THE SECOND TO TWO SIMILAR SLIDES IN THE SAME AREA.
ITD REPORTS MULTIPLE MUDSLIDES AND ROCK FALLS DUE TO EXCESSIVE RAINFALL ALONG US-93 BETWEEN EVANS ROAD AND MCKIM CREEK ROAD.
ITD REPORTS A MUDSLIDE AND WATER RUNNING ACROSS ID-75 BETWEEN EAST FORK RD AND MALM GULCH RD COMPLETELY BLOCKING THE HIGHWAY.
ITD REPORTS WATER ACROSS ID-37 BETWEEN LAWERY LN AND COW CANYON RD.

AN NWS SURVEY TEAM FOUND EVIDENCE OF AN EXTENSIVE MUD SLIDE ALONG AND PREVIOUSLY ACROSS HIGHWAY 75 NORTH OF CLAYTON. THE MUD AND DEBRIS HAD SINCE BEEN CLEANED UP BUT THE ROAD WAS GREATLY IMPACTED. A FLOOD ADVISORY WAS OUT AT THE TIME.
AFTER SEVERAL DAYS OF HEAVY RAIN, MULTIPLE ROCK/MUD SLIDES OCCURRED ALONG TRAIL CREEK ROAD CLOSING THE ROAD FOR SEVERAL DAYS. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR AND SOCIAL MEDIA.
FALLEN TREE LIMBS SUSTAINING DAMAGE TO A HOUSE WITH BRANCHES IN ROADWAYS.
MESONET STATION RFTI1 - RAFT RIVER RAWS.
PUBLIC REPORTS A MEASURED WIND GUST TO 78.7 MPH ON YALE RD JUST SOUTH OF 400 N. CORROBORATED BY NEARBY 67 MPH GUST AT THE RAFT RIVER RAWS 5 MINUTES EARLIER.

THUNDERSTORMS KNOCKED OUT POWER TO RESIDENTS SOUTH AND EAST OF ASHTON. REPORTED AROUND 900 PM. FALL RIVER RURAL ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE REPORTS POWER WAS RESTORED BY 11:11 PM.
MESONET STATION OHOI1 - OHIO GULCH.
POWERLINES DOWN.
MULTIPLE POWER OUTAGES AFFECTING OVER 500 CUSTOMERS NEAR MILNER, HOBSON, AND HYNES. A WIND GUST TO 59 MPH WAS OBSERVED NEARBY AT THE ITD RWIS I-84 VALLEY INTERCHANGE STATION AROUND THE SAME TIME.
ROCKY MOUNTAIN POWER REPORTS THUNDERSTORM WINDS KNOCKED OUT POWER TO 176 CUSTOMERS WEST OF IDAHO FALLS AIRPORT.
OLD US-91 JUST SOUTH OF US-30. VIDEO SUBMITTED TO NWS POCATELLO VIA FACEBOOK. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR. NO DAMAGE REPORTED.
ESTIMATED HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL IN THE MEADOWVILLE AREA. HAIL COVERED THE GROUND. PHOTO/VIDEO POSTED BY THE PUBLIC ON THE MICHAEL COATS KIFI FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

UPDATED TIME...ESTIMATED HALF DOLLAR SIZE HAIL IN THE MEADOWVILLE AREA. HAIL COVERED THE GROUND. PHOTO/VIDEO POSTED BY THE PUBLIC ON THE MICHAEL COATS KIFI FACEBOOK PAGE. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
UPDATED LOCATION AND TIME...SEARCH AND RESCUE MEMBER REPORTS AT LEAST QUARTER SIZE HAIL WITH A VEHICLE COVERED IN SIGNIFICANT HAIL DENTS ALONG ID-34 IN THE CONDA AREA.

UPDATED...MULTIPLE REPORTS, PHOTOS, AND VIDEOS OF HALF DOLLAR AND PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL AT ITAFOS PLANT IN CONDA. HAIL OF VARIOUS SIZES COVERED THE GROUND AND MULTIPLE VEHICLES WERE COVERED IN DENTS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.

PUBLIC SUBMITTED PHOTOS TO NWS POCATELLO OF ESTIMATED PING PONG BALL SIZE HAIL NEAR CONDA. HAIL OF VARIOUS SIZES COVERED THE GROUND. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
POWER OUTAGE REPORTED IN THE AREA WITH OVER 100 CUSTOMERS IMPACTED. GUSTS NEAR 50MPH WERE IN THE AREA AT THE TIME. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

MULTIPLE LARGE COTTONWOOD TREES DOWN. TIME ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.

POWER OUTAGES AFFECTING ALMOST 1,000 CUSTOMERS IN POCATELLO. NEARBY WEATHER STATIONS REPORTED CONVECTIVE OUTFLOW WINDS OF 45 TO 58 MPH AROUND THIS TIME.

POCATELLO REGIONAL AIRPORT. CONVECTIVE OUTFLOW WINDS. GUST CORRECTED FROM 58 MPH TO 59 MPH.
NUMEROUS BIRCH TREES AROUND 4-6 INCH DIAMETER UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. DAMAGE APPEARS TO BE CONSISTENT WITH SIGNIFICANT STRAIGTLINE WINDS ASSOCIATED WITH THE REAR FLANK DOWNDRAFT.
HAIL AROUND ONE INCH ACCUMULATED TO A DEPTH OF A FEW INCHES ACROSS THE ROADWAY AND SURROUNDING FIELDS ALONG AND AROUND I-77.
LANDSLIDE REPORTED BY KMVT IN THE AREA OF SCHIPPER CAMPGROUND, WITHIN THE BADGER FIRE BURN SCAR.
ROOF BLOWN OFF BUILDING ALONG E CEDAR ST. REPORT AND PHOTO FROM THE PUBLIC ON THE IDAHO STATE JOURNAL FACEBOOK PAGE.
MANY SHINGLES BLOWN OFF A RESIDENCE ALONG VISTA DR. EXACT TIME DAMAGE ORIGINALLY OCCURRED UNKNOWN.
